title: What causes avalanches, and can you survive them?
speaker: Simon Trautman

background.summary: 2012年カスケード山脈で雪崩に巻き込まれたプロスキーヤー Elyse Saugstad の生還を導入に、雪崩の発生条件3つ(斜度・弱層・トリガー)、速度を決める要因、史上最悪の1970年ペルー雪崩、生還を高める対策と装備までを解説する。
details: Elyse Saugstad: 2012年2月カスケード山脈での雪崩で airbag を展開して生還したプロスキーヤー。同行者3名は死亡。
雪崩発生3条件: (1) 斜度 30〜45° (積もる十分な緩さと動く十分な急さ)、(2) 強い層が弱層(surface hoar など)の上に積もる「something over nothing」、(3) 自然または人為のトリガー。
速度を左右する要因: 斜度・植生・地形粗さ・雪質。乾いた粉雪は粉塵雲を伴い高速、湿った雪雪崩は遅いがはるかに密で破壊的。大型は時速160km超。
Huascarán 雪崩 (1970年, ペルー): 氷河氷・雪・泥・岩 50〜100百万 m³ が時速300km超で町を埋め、18,000人が死亡 ── 史上最悪。
対策: スキー場・公園・幹線道路では avalanche worker が気象・積雪安定性・人間活動を常時監視、必要なら爆薬で人為的に発生させて制御。backcountry では個人責任で予報確認・訓練・救助装備携行。

paragraph_summaries_ja.summary: 2012年2月、プロスキーヤー Elyse Saugstad がカスケード山脈で5,000トンの雪壁に呑み込まれた。「雪崩!」の声を間一髪で聞き、命を救う動作で反応した。
雪崩とは雪・氷・岩などが斜面を急降下する現象。米国だけで年平均27人を殺す自然災害かつ個人的悲劇。
雪崩発生の3条件、第一は適切な斜度 ── 通常30〜45度。積もるには十分緩く、動くには十分急。
第二は強固な雪層が弱層の上に積もる「something over nothing」構造。カスケードでは新雪1mスラブの下に薄い surface hoar(表面霜)があった。
最後にトリガー。豪雪・強風・降雨・他の雪崩などの自然要因、スキーヤーやスノーモービル乗りの地表衝撃などの人為要因。破断は雪塊中を伝播するので数百m離れた地点からも起こせる。
速さは斜度・植生・地形粗さ・雪質に依存。乾いた粉雪は高速で粉塵雲を伴い、湿った雪は遅いが密で破壊的。1970年ペルーの大雪崩は5,000万〜1億m³が時速300km超で町を埋め18,000人を殺した史上最悪例。
小さくても巻き込まれれば危険。雪は固体と液体両方のように振る舞い、前線では表層が下層より速く動いて犠牲者を深部に引き込む。停止すると圧縮で身動き不能になる。
最善は巻き込まれないこと。スキー場や幹線道路では雪崩担当者が気象・安定性・人間活動を常時監視。脅威検知時は地形閉鎖や爆薬・砲撃による制御発生も行う。
管理外の backcountry は自己責任。地元雪崩センターの予報を理解し、訓練を受け、救助装備を携行する。
高リスク地形でリスクをゼロにはできない。カスケードの一行は経験豊富だったが、嵐後の高危険予報下、3人が死亡。Elyse は膨張式エアバッグを展開して生還 ── 不完全だが最後の防衛線として、犠牲者を表面に近く保つ装置。

sentences.text: In February 2012, pro skier Elyse Saugstad was carving through the slopes of the Cascade Mountains when a crack formed in the snowpack above her.
sentences.translation_ja: 2012年2月、プロスキーヤー Elyse Saugstad がカスケード山脈の斜面をターンしながら滑り降りていたとき、頭上の雪塊に亀裂が走った。

structure.label: M
structure.content: through the slopes of the Cascade Mountains

structure.label: M
structure.content: when a crack formed in the snowpack above her

sentences.text: Before she was fully engulfed by a 5,000-ton wall of snow, Elyse heard "AVALANCHE!" just in time to react with a lifesaving maneuver.
sentences.translation_ja: 5,000トンの雪の壁に完全に呑み込まれる直前、Elyse は「雪崩!」の声を間一髪で聞き、命を救う動作で反応した。

structure.label: M
structure.content: Before she was fully engulfed by a 5,000-ton wall of snow

structure.label: M
structure.content: just in time to react with a lifesaving maneuver

tokens.surface: by a 5,000-ton wall of snow, Elyse heard "

sentences.text: So, how could she survive this crushing snow surge?
sentences.translation_ja: では、彼女はどうやってこの押し潰すような雪のうねりを生き延びたのか?

sentences.text: Avalanches are the rapid descension of snow, ice, rock, or a combination of these down a slope.
sentences.translation_ja: 雪崩とは、雪・氷・岩、またはそれらの組み合わせが斜面を急降下する現象である。

structure.label: C
structure.content: the rapid descension of snow, ice, rock, or a combination of these down a slope

tokens.surface: of snow, ice, rock, or a

sentences.text: They can be destructive natural disasters and personal tragedies: in the United States alone, avalanches kill an average of 27 people annually.
sentences.translation_ja: それらは破壊的な自然災害であり個人的悲劇でもある。米国だけでも、雪崩は年平均27人を死に至らしめる。

structure.label: C
structure.content: destructive natural disasters and personal tragedies

structure.label: M
structure.content: in the United States alone, avalanches kill an average of 27 people annually

sentences.text: These alpine nightmares don't just awaken out of nowhere; they need three specific conditions.
sentences.translation_ja: これらアルプスの悪夢は何もないところから突然目覚めるわけではない。3つの特定条件を必要とする。

sentences.text: First, the mountain needs to have the right slope, typically between 30 and 45 degrees.
sentences.translation_ja: 第一に、山が適切な斜度を持つ必要があり、通常は30〜45度の間だ。

structure.label: O
structure.content: the right slope, typically between 30 and 45 degrees

tokens.surface: First, the mountain needs to have the right slope,

sentences.text: This is shallow enough to allow snow to build up on the slope, yet steep enough to provide enough potential energy for movement.
sentences.translation_ja: これは雪が斜面に積もるには十分に緩やかであり、かつ移動するに十分な位置エネルギーを与える急さでもある。

structure.label: C
structure.content: shallow enough to allow snow to build up on the slope, yet steep enough to provide enough potential energy for movement

sentences.text: Second, they typically need a layer of sturdy snow deposited over a weak or unstable layer, or what's sometimes called "something over nothing."
sentences.translation_ja: 第二に、強固な雪の層が弱いまたは不安定な層の上に積もる構造 ── 通称「something over nothing」 ── が通常必要となる。

structure.label: O
structure.content: a layer of sturdy snow deposited over a weak or unstable layer, or what's sometimes called "something over nothing"

tokens.surface: Second, they typically need a layer of

sentences.text: Take the Cascade Mountain avalanche, where recent heavy storms deposited a nearly 1-meter-deep slab of fresh snow.
sentences.translation_ja: カスケード山の雪崩を例にとると、直前の豪雪が約1メートル厚の新雪スラブを堆積させていた。

structure.label: M
structure.content: where recent heavy storms deposited a nearly 1-meter-deep slab of fresh snow

tokens.surface: Take the Cascade Mountain avalanche, where recent heavy

sentences.text: Below this sat a thin layer of surface hoar.
sentences.translation_ja: この下には薄い表面霜(サーフェスホーア)の層があった。

structure.label: S
structure.content: a thin layer of surface hoar

tokens.surface: Below this sat a thin layer of

sentences.text: These are delicate, feathery ice crystals that form when water vapor in the air rapidly freezes on the surface of snow, almost like dew in the wintertime.
sentences.translation_ja: これらは繊細で羽毛状の氷結晶で、大気中の水蒸気が雪面で急速に凍結することで形成される ── 言わば冬の露のような存在だ。

structure.label: C
structure.content: delicate, feathery ice crystals that form when water vapor in the air rapidly freezes on the surface of snow, almost like dew in the wintertime

sentences.text: Finally, avalanches need a trigger, something to collapse the weak layer and put the snow in motion.
sentences.translation_ja: 最後に、雪崩には引き金 ── 弱層を崩し雪を動かす何か ── が必要だ。

structure.label: O
structure.content: a trigger, something to collapse the weak layer and put the snow in motion

sentences.text: A trigger can be natural, such as heavy snowfall, strong winds, rainfall, or even other avalanches.
sentences.translation_ja: 引き金は自然なものでもあり得る ── 豪雪・強風・降雨、または別の雪崩でさえ。

structure.label: C
structure.content: natural, such as heavy snowfall, strong winds, rainfall, or even other avalanches

sentences.text: Or they can be human-caused, like the surface impact of skiers or snowmobilers.
sentences.translation_ja: あるいは人為的でもあり得る ── スキーヤーやスノーモービル乗りの地表衝撃のように。

structure.label: C
structure.content: human-caused, like the surface impact of skiers or snowmobilers

sentences.text: Since fractures can propagate quickly through the snowpack, triggers can originate hundreds of meters away— upstream, downstream, or even adjacent to the avalanche's path.
sentences.translation_ja: 破断は雪塊中を急速に伝播しうるため、引き金は数百メートル離れた地点から発生することもある ── 上流・下流、さらには雪崩の進路の隣からさえも。

structure.label: M
structure.content: Since fractures can propagate quickly through the snowpack

structure.label: M
structure.content: hundreds of meters away— upstream, downstream, or even adjacent to the avalanche's path

tokens.surface: quickly through the snowpack, triggers can

sentences.text: Once in motion, how quickly an avalanche moves depends on the slope's steepness, its exposure or vegetation cover, terrain roughness, and the type and amount of snow involved.
sentences.translation_ja: ひとたび動き出すと、雪崩の速さは斜度・露出度や植生被覆・地形の粗さ・関与する雪の種類と量に依存する。

structure.label: M
structure.content: on the slope's steepness, its exposure or vegetation cover, terrain roughness, and the type and amount of snow involved

tokens.surface: , how quickly an avalanche moves depends on the slope's

sentences.text: For example, dry, light, and loosely bonded snow tends to travel fast, forming a powder cloud as it moves.
sentences.translation_ja: 例えば乾いた軽い緩く結合した雪は高速で進む傾向があり、動くにつれて粉塵雲を形成する。

structure.label: S
structure.content: dry, light, and loosely bonded snow

structure.label: M
structure.content: fast, forming a powder cloud as it moves

sentences.text: In contrast, wet snow avalanches, caused by water moving through the snowpack, travel more slowly but are much denser and can be particularly destructive.
sentences.translation_ja: 対照的に湿った雪雪崩は、雪塊中を水が移動することで生じ、進行は遅いが密度がずっと高く、特に破壊的になりうる。

structure.label: S
structure.content: wet snow avalanches, caused by water moving through the snowpack

structure.label: C
structure.content: more slowly but are much denser and can be particularly destructive

tokens.surface: , caused by water moving through the snowpack, travel more slowly but are much

sentences.text: Larger avalanches can travel more than 160 kilometers per hour, breaking trees, destroying buildings, and burying roadways.
sentences.translation_ja: より大きな雪崩は時速160kmを超え、木をなぎ倒し、建物を破壊し、道路を埋め尽くす。

structure.label: M
structure.content: more than 160 kilometers per hour, breaking trees, destroying buildings, and burying roadways

tokens.surface: can travel more than 160 kilometers per hour, breaking trees,

sentences.text: In 1970, one particularly massive avalanche consisting of 50 to 100 million cubic meters of glacial ice, snow, mud, and rock reached speeds of over 300 kilometers per hour.
sentences.translation_ja: 1970年、5,000万〜1億立方メートルの氷河氷・雪・泥・岩から成る特に巨大な雪崩は、時速300kmを超えた。

structure.label: S
structure.content: one particularly massive avalanche consisting of 50 to 100 million cubic meters of glacial ice, snow, mud, and rock

structure.label: O
structure.content: speeds of over 300 kilometers per hour

tokens.surface: ice, snow, mud, and rock reached

sentences.text: This avalanche proved to be the deadliest in history, engulfing an entire Peruvian town and killing 18,000 people.
sentences.translation_ja: この雪崩は史上最も致命的なものとなり、ペルーの一町を丸ごと呑み込み18,000人を殺した。

structure.label: M
structure.content: engulfing an entire Peruvian town and killing 18,000 people

sentences.text: Avalanches aren't always this large, but even smaller ones can be dangerous for anyone caught in their path.
sentences.translation_ja: 雪崩は常にこれほど大きいわけではないが、小さなものでも進路に巻き込まれた者には危険となる。

structure.label: M
structure.content: but even smaller ones can be dangerous for anyone caught in their path

tokens.surface: aren't always this large, but even smaller ones can be

sentences.text: As snow rushes downhill, it can behave like both a solid and a liquid, making its movement difficult to predict.
sentences.translation_ja: 雪が斜面を駆け下りるとき、固体と液体の両方のように振る舞いうるため、動きの予測が難しくなる。

structure.label: C
structure.content: like both a solid and a liquid

structure.label: M
structure.content: making its movement difficult to predict

sentences.text: At the fastest-moving front, surface snow moves faster than the snow beneath, pulling victims deep into the snowpack and scattering them across wide areas, making rescue difficult.
sentences.translation_ja: 最速の前線では表層雪が下層より速く動き、犠牲者を雪塊深部へ引き込み広範囲にまき散らすため、救助が難しくなる。

structure.label: M
structure.content: faster than the snow beneath, pulling victims deep into the snowpack and scattering them across wide areas, making rescue difficult

tokens.surface: snow moves faster than the snow

sentences.text: Once an avalanche slows, the snow loses its fluid-like properties and compacts, leaving victims immobilized and unable to escape on their own.
sentences.translation_ja: 雪崩が遅くなると、雪は流体的性質を失い圧縮され、犠牲者は身動きできず自力脱出不能となる。

structure.label: M
structure.content: leaving victims immobilized and unable to escape on their own

tokens.surface: Once an avalanche slows, the snow loses its

sentences.text: For these reasons, the best way to survive an avalanche is to avoid one altogether.
sentences.translation_ja: これらの理由から、雪崩を生き延びる最善策は、そもそも巻き込まれないことだ。

structure.label: S
structure.content: the best way to survive an avalanche

tokens.surface: , the best way to survive an avalanche is to avoid one

sentences.text: Thankfully, at most ski resorts, parks, and even highways, avalanche workers mitigate risk by continuously monitoring the weather, stability of the snowpack, and human activity.
sentences.translation_ja: 幸い、ほとんどのスキー場・公園・幹線道路で、雪崩担当者が気象・雪塊安定性・人間活動を常時監視してリスクを軽減している。

structure.label: M
structure.content: Thankfully, at most ski resorts, parks, and even highways

structure.label: M
structure.content: by continuously monitoring the weather, stability of the snowpack, and human activity

sentences.text: If a threat is detected, they'll close terrain and may even intentionally trigger a controlled avalanche with explosives or artillery.
sentences.translation_ja: 脅威が検知されれば、地形を閉鎖し、爆薬や砲撃で制御下の雪崩を意図的に発生させることもある。

sentences.text: However, there are regions where avalanche risk is not actively mitigated, known as the backcountry.
sentences.translation_ja: しかし、雪崩リスクが積極的には軽減されていない地域があり、それは backcountry(バックカントリー)と呼ばれる。

structure.label: C
structure.content: regions where avalanche risk is not actively mitigated, known as the backcountry

sentences.text: Here, you are responsible for managing your own risk.
sentences.translation_ja: ここでは、自分でリスクを管理する責任を負う。

structure.label: C
structure.content: responsible for managing your own risk

sentences.text: Local avalanche centers provide avalanche forecasts, and anyone entering the backcountry should understand them, be properly trained, and carry essential rescue gear.
sentences.translation_ja: 地元の雪崩センターが雪崩予報を提供しており、backcountry に入る者は予報を理解し、適切に訓練を受け、必須の救助装備を携行すべきだ。

structure.label: S
structure.content: Local avalanche centers / anyone entering the backcountry

structure.label: V
structure.content: provide / should understand … be trained … carry

structure.label: O
structure.content: avalanche forecasts / them, essential rescue gear

tokens.surface: , and anyone entering the backcountry should understand them, be

sentences.text: And ultimately, in high-hazard terrain, there's no way to completely eliminate risk.
sentences.translation_ja: そして究極的に、高リスクの地形ではリスクを完全に排除する手段はない。

structure.label: C
structure.content: no way to completely eliminate risk

tokens.surface: terrain, there's no way to completely

sentences.text: The group at the Cascade Mountains consisted of highly experienced skiers.
sentences.translation_ja: カスケード山脈のグループは、極めて経験豊富なスキーヤーから成っていた。

structure.label: S
structure.content: The group at the Cascade Mountains

tokens.surface: The group at the Cascade Mountains

sentences.text: Yet the area was very exposed, and following a large storm, the avalanche danger forecast was elevated.
sentences.translation_ja: しかし地域は極めて露出していて、大きな嵐の後で雪崩危険度の予報は上がっていた。

structure.label: M
structure.content: and following a large storm, the avalanche danger forecast was elevated

sentences.text: In the end, three skiers lost their lives.
sentences.translation_ja: 結局、3人のスキーヤーが命を落とした。

sentences.text: But not Elyse, as she was able to deploy an inflatable airbag.
sentences.translation_ja: しかし Elyse は別だった。膨張式エアバッグを展開できたからだ。

structure.label: M
structure.content: as she was able to deploy an inflatable airbag

tokens.surface: But not Elyse, as she was able to

sentences.text: These devices are imperfect and a last line of defense, yet can keep a victim closer to the surface, improving their chance of rescue should disaster strike.
sentences.translation_ja: これらの装置は不完全で最後の防衛線だが、犠牲者を表面に近く保ち、災害発生時の救助機会を高めうる。

structure.label: C
structure.content: imperfect and a last line of defense / a victim closer to the surface

structure.label: M
structure.content: improving their chance of rescue should disaster strike

example.ja: バックカントリーを滑る。

example.ja: 無謀なスノーモービラー。
